idea-plugin.name: CodeAtlas
idea-plugin.description:
Natural-language code navigation for IntelliJ IDEA.

Ask questions like "where is authentication implemented?" or "how does the payment flow work?" and jump directly to the matching class, method, or top-level function.

How it works

  • Search runs fully offline. A small embedding model (BGE-small INT8, ~33 MB) is bundled with the plugin and runs in the IDE process. No API key or network connection is required for search.
  • Optional answers with citations. Click Ask to get a streamed natural-language answer that cites the source code it quotes. Bring your own LLM provider — Anthropic, OpenAI, or a local Ollama instance. API keys live in IntelliJ's PasswordSafe and are never sent anywhere except your chosen provider.
  • Right-click integration. Select code in the editor, then Ask CodeAtlas to search for the selection or the symbol at the caret without leaving the file.

Supported languages

Kotlin and Java in this release. The LanguageAdapter layer is designed to accept more languages in future versions.

action.description: Search CodeAtlas for the selected text, or for the symbol at the caret.
Open the CodeAtlas tool window and focus the search field.
Force a fresh full reindex of the open project.
Delete the persisted index cache and rebuild from scratch.
